system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight section (along x-axis)
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    // Block 1 - bend connection region
    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Inner corner point
    (  25  -50  25 )  // 8
    (  25  -50 -25 )  // 9

    // Block 2 - outlet straight section (along -y axis)
    (  25  -75  25 )  // 10
    (  25  -75 -25 )  // 11
    (  25 -125 -25 )  // 12
    (  25 -125  25 )  // 13
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    // Bottom face (z=-25): 0,1,2,3 should wind CCW when viewed from +z
    // That means 0(-50,25,-25), 1(-50,25,25), 2(-50,-25,25), 3(-50,-25,-25)
    // Top face (z=25): 3,2,6,7 should wind CCW when viewed from +z
    // That means 3(-50,-25,25), 2(-50,-25,-25), 6(0,-25,-25), 7(0,-25,25)
    // Looking from +x, bottom face 0->1->2->3 is CW, so reverse to 0->3->2->1
    // Similarly top face 4->5->6->7 from +x is CW, so reverse to 4->7->6->5
    // But for hex, we need bottom face (z-min) and top face (z-max)
    // z-min face: 0,1,2,3 (but need CCW from +x: 0,3,2,1)
    // z-max face: 4,5,6,7 (need CCW from +x: 4,7,6,5)
    // So hex(bottom_face top_face) = hex(0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    // Connect point 4(0,25,25) to inner corner 8(25,-50,25)
    // Bottom face at y=-50: 9(25,-50,-25), 8(25,-50,25), 10(25,0,25), 11(25,0,-25)
    // Top face at y=25: 4(0,25,25), 5(0,25,-25), 7(0,-25,25), 6(0,-25,-25)
    // For hex, bottom face (more negative y) should be first
    // y-min face: 9,8,10,11 (y=-50)
    // y-max face: 4,5,7,6 (y=25,-25)
    // From +z, bottom face 9->8->10->11 is CW, so reverse to 9->11->10->8
    // From +z, top face 4->5->7->6 is CW, so reverse to 4->6->7->5
    hex (9 11 10 8 4 6 7 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    // Extending in -y from y=-75 to y=-125
    // y-min face (z varies): 11(25,-75,-25), 10(25,-75,25), 13(25,-125,25), 12(25,-125,-25)
    // y-max face: 12(25,-125,-25), 13(25,-125,25), 10(25,-75,25), 11(25,-75,-25)
    // From +z, bottom face 11->10->13->12 is CW, so reverse to 11->12->13->10
    hex (11 12 13 10) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 10 11)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 external faces
            (0 3 7 1)  // front (z=25)
            (3 2 6 7)  // back (z=-25)
            (0 4 5 1)  // top (y=25)
            (1 5 6 2)  // bottom (y=-25, but this is internal with block1? No, block0 bottom is y=-25 at x=0)

            // Block 1 external faces
            (9 8 6 11)  // front (z=25)
            (8 10 7 6)  // back (z=-25)
            (9 11 5 4)  // top (y=25)
            (11 4 7 10)  // bottom (y=-50)

            // Block 2 external faces
            (11 12 13 10)  // front (z=25)
            (12 13 7 6)  // back (z=-25)
            (12 11 5 4)  // top (y=-75)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points" file

This is a cascading error. Since `blockMesh` previously failed (inside-out blocks), no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files. However, looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the block definitions appear to have been fixed from Attempt 2's `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` to what looks like correct winding. Let me re-examine the current state.

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict` more carefully:

### Block 0: `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)`
Vertices:
- Face 0: 0(-50,25,25), 3(-50,-25,25), 2(-50,-25,-25), 1(-50,25,-25) — z-min face
- Face 1: 4(0,25,25), 7(0,-25,25), 6(0,-25,-25), 5(0,25,-25) — z-max face

Viewing face 0 from +x: 0→3→2→1 winds CCW ✓  
Viewing face 1 from +x: 4→7→6→5 winds CCW ✓  
This should be a valid block.

### Block 1: `hex (9 11 10 8 4 6 7 5)`
Face 0: 9(25,-50,-25), 11(25,0,-25), 10(25,-75,-25), 8(25,-50,25) — y-min face  
Face 1: 4(0,25,25), 6(0,-25,25), 7(0,-25,-25), 5(0,25,-25) — y-max face

There's a **serious geometric problem**: face 1 uses vertices 4,6,7,5 which are NOT coplanar! Vertex 4 is at (0,25,25), vertex 6 at (0,-25,25), vertex 7 at (0,-25,-25), vertex 5 at (0,25,-25). These form a tetrahedron-like shape, not a flat face. Additionally, vertex 8 at (25,-50,25) and vertex 9 at (25,-50,-25) are at x=25, completely disconnected from the rest of the geometry.

Similarly, **Block 2** `hex (11 12 13 10)` only has 4 vertices — this is malformed syntax. A hex block requires exactly 8 vertices.

## Root Cause

The vertex numbering and block topology were designed incorrectly. The bend geometry connects blocks that share faces, but the current vertex assignments don't create shared faces between blocks. Each block references vertices that are geometrically incompatible.

## Recommended Solution — Completely Rewrite `blockMeshDict`

Adopt the **exact vertex layout and block topology from the official squareBend tutorial**. Based on the user requirement mentioning vertices at (-50, 25, 25), (-50, 25, -25), (25, 0, 25), (25, 0, -25), and outlet points, here is a corrected approach:

### Step 1: Define vertices properly for a propertly connected multi-block mesh

```
vertices
(
    // Left face (x = -50)
    (-50,  25,  25)  // 0
    (-50,  25, -25)  // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)  // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)  // 3

    // Right face of block 0 (x = 0, y = 25)
    (0,    25,  25)  // 4
    (0,    25, -25)  // 5

    // Inner corner face (x = 0, y = -50)
    (0,   -50,  25)  // 6
    (0,   -50, -25)  // 7

    // Outer corner face (x = 25, y = -50)
    (25,  -50,  25)  // 8
    (25,  -50, -25)  // 9

    // Right face of block 1 (x = 25, y = -75)
    (25,  -75,  25)  // 10
    (25,  -75, -25)  // 11

    // Far end (x = 25, y = -125)
    (25, -125,  25)  // 12
    (25, -125, -25)  // 13
);
```

### Step 2: Define blocks with proper shared-face connectivity

```
blocks
(
    // Block 0: Straight inlet section (x=-50 to x=0, y=25 to y=-25)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend transition (x=0 to x=25, y=-50 to y=-75)
    hex (4 5 7 6 8 9 11 10) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section (x=25, y=-75 to y=-125)
    hex (8 9 11 12 8 9 11 12) -- WRONG, needs 8 distinct vertices
    // Actually Block 2 extends downward in y from y=-75 to y=-125 at x=25
    // Need new vertices beyond 13, or restructure differently
);
```

Actually, the simplest fix: **remove Block 2 entirely** and extend Block 1 to cover the full outlet section. Or better yet, **restructure the entire mesh as a single L-shaped block** using fewer vertices.

### Best Approach: Single-Block L-Shaped Mesh

Given the complexity and the fact that previous attempts keep failing, define a **minimal valid mesh**:

```
vertices
(
    // Origin of L-joint
    (0, 0, 0)    // 0
    (0, 25, 0)   // 1
    (0, 25, 25)  // 2
    (0, 25, -25) // 3
    (0, -25, 0)  // 4
    (0, -25, 25) // 5
    (0, -25, -25)// 6
    (25, 0, 0)   // 7
    (25, 0, 25)  // 8
    (25, 0, -25) // 9
    (-50, 0, 0)  // 10
    (-50, 25, 25)// 11
    (-50, 25, -25)// 12
);

blocks
(
    // Main duct: x=-50 to x=0, y=-25 to y=25 (rectangular cross-section)
    hex (10 11 12 6 7 8 9 4) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Bend duct: x=0 to x=25, y=0 to y=-25
    hex (7 8 9 4 10 11 12 6) -- WRONG
);
```

This is getting overly complex. **The cleanest solution:**

## Final Recommendation

Rewrite `blockMeshDict` to match the **official squareBend tutorial exactly**. Use only 4 vertices for a simple straight duct, or adopt the proven 20+ vertex multi-block topology from the actual tutorial. The key principle:

1. Every hex block must have 8 vertices where each pair of opposite faces are planar quadrilaterals.
2. Opposite faces must wind in the same direction (right-hand rule).
3. Shared faces between adjacent blocks must have identical vertex ordering.
4. All boundary patch names in `blockMeshDict` must match those referenced in `0/T`, `0/U`, `0/p`.

Specifically, copy the vertex coordinates and block definitions **verbatim** from the working squareBend tutorial, then only modify the `controlDict`, `fvSchemes`, `fvSolution`, and initial/boundary condition files to match the user requirements.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
